decoded.explanation: The defendant committed a significant breach of trust by embezzling $25,000 from their employer. While white-collar crimes of this nature warrant a custodial sentence to serve as a deterrent, several mitigating factors are present in this case. The defendant has no prior criminal record and has already demonstrated a preliminary willingness to make amends by partially reimbursing the stolen funds. Balancing the severity of the financial theft and the abuse of a professional position against these mitigating circumstances, a brief term of incarceration is appropriate. This sentence aims to provide accountability while allowing the defendant to ultimately return to society and complete full restitution.
